description: Business letter in Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: 11th or 12th century. Mentions places such as al-Mahdiyya and Alexandria; people such as Abū Sulaymān Daʾūd Lebdī (cf. CUL Or.1080 J92 (PGPID 5390)); Ibn Qayyūma; and goods such as red pomegranate seed and nutmeg. Mentions concern about the arrival of something "for the east winds are many and promiscuous" (פאן אלשראקי כתירה זאניה). (Information in part from CUDL)
